creator: Pandosy, Charles Marie, 1824-1901
Shea, John Gilmary, 1824-1892
Gibbs, George, 1815-1873
description: 100 copies printed and signed in ms. hand by J.G. Shea. Cf. p. [vi]

"During the Indian war in Oregon and Washington territory, the original of the grammar was lost, and the following translation made some time previously alone remained. A revision of the dictionary, much more extensive than the accompanying, was destroyed at the same time."--Pref

Includes a "Grammar of the P'shwan-Wa-Pam, Wala-Wala, Tairtla, Roil-Roil-Pam and Palus languages", p. 9-30; The Lord's Prayer in Yakama, p. 31; "Peace song composed by Father Pandosy", to the tune of the Marseillaise (without music), p. 32-34; dictionary, p. 35-59
John Carter Brown Library copy has no. 14 of 100
subject: Yakama language
Indian linguistics
Imprint 1862
title: Grammar and dictionary of the Yakama language.
